old_claim.note: Drop Spindles provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct drop-spindle dependency remains because dyed textiles require a textile substrate, but not that the yarn was made by a specific spindle technology.

source_shape.source_locator: Britannica's textile article supports the textile substrate and dyeing/coloring scope without making drop spindles the required production technology.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports textiles and dyeing broadly, not drop-spindle manufacture as a direct prerequisite.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The retained textiles_weaving edge captures the substrate; a spindle-specific edge would overconstrain dyeing to one yarn-production method.

ontology_before: Dyed Textiles depended on Drop Spindles as an unsourced enabling edge.
ontology_after: Dyed Textiles depends on Textiles & Weaving without requiring a specific spinning tool.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: drop_spindles is no longer a direct prerequisite for dyed_textiles.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to dyed spun yarn rather than dyed fibers and cloth.
A stronger source showed the first dyed textiles specifically required drop-spindle yarn production.
A separate dyed_spun_yarn node were added and scoped to spindle-made yarn.
short_reason: The textile substrate matters; the spindle tool is over-specific.
